subject: 📊 Daily Portfolio: -2.2% | AVGO stop hit — semi complex under pressure
html_body:
Daily Portfolio Review

Tuesday June 24, 2026 · Prices as of NYSE close June 23 · Kuwait 6:00 AM

PORTFOLIO VALUE

$828.39

▼ -$18.20 today (-2.18%)

Cost basis $614.95  ·  P&L -$0.37 (-0.06%)

🎯 Withdrawal Goal

$828 / $1,000

$171.61 to go · 82.8%

⚠️ NVDA Jun 24 early session: $200-204 — stop at $204 is in real-time danger. China AI chip loophole tightening (DoC action Jun 23) continuing to weigh. Monitor the open closely.

📊 Positions Snapshot

STATUSTICKERCLOSEDAYP&L $P&L %
⚠️ WATCHNVDA$208.65-2.79%-$12.14-4.05%
✅ HOLDLLY$1,107.08-0.37%+$11.05+11.26%
🚨 ALERTAVGO$380.15-3.10%-$10.04-7.73%
⚠️ WATCHTSM$436.39-5.57%+$10.76+12.37%
TOTAL (positions)$614.58-2.96%-$0.37-0.06%

Sources: Yahoo Finance · CNBC · Nasdaq · Gurufocus · StockTitan. Jun 23 close. Last portfolio update: Jun 9, 2026.

🚨 Exit Alerts

🚨 AVGO — STOP ESSENTIALLY HIT · $380.15 vs stop $379.00 (0.3% above)

Why the stop is real: SK Hynix announced June 23 it is deliberately slowing HBM4 production to redirect capacity into conventional DRAM. This hits AVGO's AI networking revenue narrative. The June 5 post-earnings floor at $386.62 has been definitively broken. Real next support: $370-375 (Feb 2026 breakout level). Staying in means risking another -$3/share down to $375.

Stop derivation: $379 was placed just below the June 5 earnings-day floor. That floor broke on June 23 — the stop is correctly triggered. Execute at open.

Recommended: EXIT 0.315 shares AVGO at open

Gross proceeds: 0.315 × $380.15 = $119.75

Cost basis: $129.79

Gross P&L: -$10.04  |  Exit commission: -$3.00  |  Net P&L: -$13.04

Net cash received: $116.75 → total cash becomes $330.56

Your call. Stop was placed for exactly this scenario. Execute.

⚠️ NVDA — 2.3% ABOVE STOP · CHINA CHIP LOOPHOLE HEADWIND

Close $208.65. Stop: $204.00. Jun 24 early session trading $200-204 — the stop is live. US DoC moved to close the offshore-subsidiary loophole that allowed Rubin/Blackwell chips to reach Chinese AI firms. China demand was a key bull-case component.

Stop derivation: $204 sits just below the May 2026 pre-breakout consolidation band ($204-208) and the 200-day MA cluster (~$189-215). Intraday Jun 24 has touched $200.04 — the pre-breakout base. A close below $204 on volume = thesis broken, exit immediately.

DO NOT exit pre-emptively — let the $204 stop work.

If stop triggers: 1.3799 × $204 = $281.50 gross | Cost basis $300.06 | Gross: -$18.56 | Commission: -$3.00 | Net: -$21.56

Q2 FY27 earnings catalyst Aug 19 is intact. Hold if stop holds. Exit if it breaks.

⚠️ TSM — 1.5% ABOVE TRAILING STOP · TP1 ZONE

Close $436.39. Trailing stop: $430.00. TP1 was $435 — already in TP1 territory. June 23 drop of -5.57% was broad semi-sector sympathy (China/HBM narrative), not TSM-specific. Foundry AI wafer thesis intact.

Partial trim blocked: 0.112 shares × ($436.39-$388.35) = $5.38 gross - $3.00 commission = $2.38 net. Proceeds $48.88 < $75 threshold. No partial trim.

Recommendation: HOLD with $430 trailing stop. TP2 $485 achievable.

Full exit at $436: 0.224 × ($436.39-$388.35) = $10.76 gross - $3.00 = +$7.76 net. Optional if you want to simplify.

Analyst targets: Barclays $470 · Needham $480 · Susquehanna $575. Stop at $430 protects the $10.76 gain.

✅ LLY — HOLD · APPROACHING TP1 (COMMISSION BLOCKS TRIM)

Close $1,107.08. TP1: $1,114 ($6.92 away, 0.6%). Stop: $915 (21.5% cushion — very safe). Orforglipron (Foundayo) approved and launched. GLP-1 duopoly intact.

Commission math for 30% trim: 0.02958 shares × ($1,107.08-$994.87) = $3.32 gross - $3.00 commission = +$0.32 net. Still blocked. Hold to TP2 $1,243.

TP derivation: $1,114 = analyst cluster top (multiple firms). TP2 $1,243 = median consensus. TP3 $1,393 = bull case. Hold.

⏳ Pending Watchlist — Waiting on IBKR Approval

VRDN Viridian TherapeuticsWATCH6/10

🔒 IBKR application pending — current broker does not allow this stock

Current: $16.63 (Jun 18 — last confirmed)Entry target: $20.00 → ⚠️ -16.9% below target
Stop: $14.50  |  TP1: $28.00  |  TP2: $37.00

⚠️ Entry geometry changed: VRDN has moved from $20.00 entry target to $16.63 (-16.9%) while waiting on broker. Original stop $14.50 is now only 12.8% below current price. Re-run analyze VRDN to update entry/stop/TPs before entering.

🔴 URGENT: PDUFA June 30 — 6 DAYS LEFT

Veligrotug for Thyroid Eye Disease (TED). BLA Priority Review + Breakthrough Therapy Designation. Phase 3 REVEAL-1 positive. Also: REVEAL-2 Phase 3 topline positive (May 5, 2026).

APPROVAL PROBABILITY: 82%

Halal: ⚠️ UNVERIFIED — check Musaffa (interest income vs milestone revenue ratio unclear)

🚨 Only 6 days to PDUFA — if IBKR not approved in time, this entry window closes June 30. Escalate application now if interested.

VERA Vera TherapeuticsBUY NOW7/10

🔒 IBKR application pending — current broker does not allow this stock

Current: ~$34.26 (Jun 23)Entry target: $34.04 → ✅ Entry valid (+0.65%)
Stop: $24.50  |  TP1: $55.00  |  TP2: $78.00

🟡 SOON: PDUFA July 7 — 13 DAYS LEFT

Atacicept (dual BAFF/APRIL) for IgA Nephropathy. Priority Review. FDA aligned on earlier ORIGIN 3 eGFR analysis (Q3 2026). Proteinuria -46% vs placebo published in NEJM. Commercial team launch-ready mid-2026.

APPROVAL PROBABILITY: 89%

Halal: ⚠️ UNVERIFIED — pre-revenue biotech, term debt structure needs Musaffa check

Entry window open at $34.26 — essentially at target. Only blocker: IBKR approval.

When approved: enter $240 at ~$34.26 (7 shares + $3 commission = $242.82 total). Stop $24.50 (-28.5%). TP1 $55 (+60.6%). 13 days to decision — still comfortable window.

📡 Catalyst Plays — June 24, 2026

Live-scanned: FDA calendar, biotech readout trackers, phase 3 monitors.

UNCYUnicycive TherapeuticsWATCH — 3/5

🔴 URGENT: NDA PDUFA June 27 — 3 DAYS

Oxylanthanum carbonate (OLC) for hyperphosphatemia in CKD patients on dialysis. Class II resubmission after prior Complete Response Letter (manufacturing issue). FDA accepted Jan 29, 2026. No AdCom scheduled.

Market cap: ~$183MCurrent: ~$6.90Float: est. ~$170M

PROBABILITY ESTIMATE: ~55%

Class II resubmission base rate ~60%, discounted for prior CRL. No novel mechanism risk. Manufacturing fix is the crux.

Scenarios (on $200 position ~29 shares):

✅ Approved: ~$11-14 (+60-100%) — prior CRL overhang lifts

💀 Rejected: ~$3-4 (-55-60%) — another CRL is existential

EV: 55% × +$116 − 45% × -$116 = +$64 − $52 = +$12 EV

Halal: ⚠️ UNVERIFIED — verify Musaffa before entering. Smart money: no visible signal.

Say 'analyze UNCY' for full deep dive before acting.

VRDNViridian Therapeutics (also in watchlist above)INVESTIGATE — 4/5

🔴 URGENT: BLA PDUFA June 30 — 6 DAYS

PROBABILITY: 82%

✅ Approved: ~$28-34 (+68-104% from $16.63)

💀 Rejected: ~$7-9 (-55-60%)

EV on $240 position: 82% × +$139 − 18% × -$143 = +$114 − $26 = +$88 EV

Halal: ⚠️ UNVERIFIED  |  IBKR blocker — escalate application urgently. 6 days left.

Say 'analyze VRDN' for the full 8-section deep dive.

VERAVera Therapeutics (also in watchlist above)INVESTIGATE — 4/5

🟡 SOON: BLA PDUFA July 7 — 13 DAYS

PROBABILITY: 89%

✅ Approved: ~$55 (+60.6%) — IgAN approval comps solid

💀 Rejected: ~$14-18 (-50-60%)

EV on $240 position (7 shares): 89% × +$145 − 11% × -$141 = +$129 − $16 = +$113 EV

Halal: ⚠️ UNVERIFIED  |  IBKR blocker. Entry window open now at $34.26.

Say 'analyze VERA' for the full 8-section deep dive.

💵 Cash Deployment

Current cash: $213.81  ·  Reserve: $6  ·  Net deployable: $204.81

If AVGO exit executes: cash rises to $330.56, deployable $321.56 after commission.

Recommendation: HOLD CASH — dry powder posture.

With NVDA touching its stop and TSM 1.5% above its stop, this is not the moment to open new positions. Two simultaneous stop scenarios = defensive cash allocation, not deployment.

UNCY (PDUFA Jun 27): 55% odds, halal unverified, 3 days notice — too speculative to enter blind.

Best option IF IBKR clears in time: VERA at ~$34.26

7 shares × $34.26 = $239.82 + $3 commission = $242.82. Stop $24.50 (-28.5%). TP1 $55 (+60.6%). PDUFA July 7 (13 days). EV: +$113. Verify halal on Musaffa first. This is only Option A — current cash is sufficient even without the AVGO exit.

🔄 Rotation

🔄 AVGO Exit → Cash Hold → VERA (if IBKR clears)

Sell: 0.315 AVGO at open (~$380)  |  Net proceeds after commission: $116.75

Hold as cash (total $330.56) while IBKR resolves.

If IBKR clears before July 7: Deploy ~$240 into VERA at ~$34.26 (89% PDUFA, +$113 EV). Keep $90 reserve.

Risk: VERA is binary — -50% if rejected. IBKR may not clear in time for VRDN (Jun 30 — only 6 days).

Execute AVGO stop first. Rotation step only if IBKR clears. Your call.

Anti-rotation rules in effect: NVDA near stop (do not pre-emptively exit); TSM not a rotation candidate (gains intact, $430 stop holds); LLY hold to TP2 (commission math blocks trim).

🎯 Position Risk Scores

TICKERSCORELABELDRIVER
NVDA65/100HIGH48% concentration + China export risk + stop proximity
LLY38/100MODERATEGLP-1 duopoly intact, Foundayo launched, strong FCF
AVGO72/100EXTREMEStop hit · HBM slowdown · -15% from high · exit signal
TSM52/100HIGHGeopolitical risk + -5.57% Jun 23 + stop at $430

📚 Today's Concept — Day 26 of 30

EV/EBITDA Part 2: The honest calculation

The traps start before you touch the ratio. AVGO reports adjusted EBITDA that excludes stock-based compensation — the adjusted number looks ~25% better than GAAP. Real calc: operating income + D&A. That is apples-to-apples. Second trap: EV needs to include all debt, minority interests, and preferred stock. AVGO carries ~$68B in long-term debt; cheap screeners that use only market cap minus cash understate the real EV by a third. Third trap: strip one-time restructuring charges and M&A integration costs from EBITDA before comparing periods — Broadcom does meaningful acquisition integration every year and those charges swing EBITDA quarter-to-quarter. Why this matters for today's exit: even on clean EV/EBITDA AVGO trades ~25x forward — a premium that only holds if the AI revenue narrative stays intact. The moment the narrative cracks (SK Hynix HBM slowdown is the first crack), the multiple compresses and you lose on both EPS and P/E simultaneously. The stop did its job.

Also today: EBITDA margin — EBITDA divided by revenue. AVGO's is ~60%, exceptional by any standard. But a 60% margin on decelerating AI revenue is still a declining absolute profit number. Margin alone does not protect you when the top-line narrative breaks.

Research only — not financial advice. Prices from NYSE close June 23, 2026 (Kuwait morning June 24). Sources: Yahoo Finance · CNBC · Nasdaq · Gurufocus · StockTitan · TradingKey · MarketBeat. Portfolio last updated: June 9, 2026.

NVDA: $208.65 (Nasdaq, Jun 23) · LLY: $1,107.08 (Nasdaq, Jun 23) · AVGO: $380.15 (Gurufocus/StockStory, Jun 23) · TSM: $436.39 (TradingKey, Jun 23, -5.57%) · VRDN: $16.63 (MarketBeat, Jun 18, last confirmed) · VERA: ~$34.26 (CNBC, Jun 23)
